祖.origin: The 礻 (spirit/altar) side honors those before us, so 祖 means ancestor — family we remember.

政.origin: 正 (upright/correct) plus 攵 (a tapping hand) means setting things right — that's government and politics.

治.origin: Water 氵 needs a wise plan 台 to guide it — so 治 means to manage and govern.

sentence.cn: 大禹治水的故事很有名。
sentence.en: The story of Yu controlling the floods is famous.

却.origin: 去 (to go away) beside a kneeling 卩 (seal) — picture turning back, so 却 means 'but' or 'yet'.

sentence.en: It got dark, but he was still playing.

灭.origin: A lid 一 pressed down on a fire 火 — cover the flame and it goes out, so 灭 means to extinguish.

初.origin: A knife 刀 cutting cloth 衤 is the very first step in sewing, so 初 means beginning.

sentence.en: This is my first time coming to Beijing.

投.origin: A hand 扌 swinging hard — picture an arm tossing a ball, so 投 means to throw or pitch.

悲.origin: Below 非 (not, opposite) sits the heart 心 — a heart turned the wrong way feels sad, so 悲 means sorrow.

划.origin: Think of a paddle slicing through water in long strokes — 划 means to row or paddle a boat.

sentence.en: We go rowing a boat on the lake.

寻.origin: A hand 彐 and a measuring inch 寸 reaching out and feeling around — that's 寻, to search and seek.

sentence.en: I am looking for my cat.

伤.origin: A person 亻 who has been hurt — 伤 means to injure or a wound.

纪.origin: Silk thread 纟 kept neat and in order — 纪 means discipline, and also an age or era.

sentence.en: He is very young in age.

念.origin: Keep the present 今 close to your heart 心 — 念 means to think of and to miss someone.

德.origin: Walk 彳 with a straight eye and an honest heart 心 — that good path is 德, virtue.

sentence.en: She is a person with good morals.

毕.origin: Picture finishing every last task on the list — 毕 means to complete or to end.

sentence.en: My brother graduates from college this year.

战.origin: 占 (to occupy) beside 戈 (a spear) — soldiers fighting for a place, so 战 means war and battle.

领.origin: 令 (command) beside 页 (a head) — the head that gives orders, so 领 means to lead, and also a collar.

sentence.en: The teacher leads us into the classroom.

务.origin: Think of getting busy with a strong effort 力 — 务 means tasks, duties, and business.

尊.origin: A hand 寸 lifting a jar of wine 酋 up high as a gift — that's 尊, to honor and respect.

敬.origin: 苟 (careful) beside 攵 (an action hand) — acting with care toward others is 敬, to respect.

sentence.en: We respect and love our teacher.

束.origin: Wood 木 wrapped tight by a tie 口 in the middle — 束 means to bind, and also a bundle.

sentence.en: He gave Mom a bunch of flowers.

贡.origin: 工 (work) over 贝 (shell money) — work and treasure given to others, so 贡 means to offer and contribute.

sentence.en: He made a contribution for everyone.

献.origin: In old days a dog 犬 was offered as a gift — 献 means to offer up and present.

sentence.en: The children present flowers to the guests.

誉.origin: Lifted-up 兴 words 言 of praise — when people speak well of you, that's 誉, fame and good name.

sentence.en: He cares a lot about his reputation.

培.origin: Earth 土 piled around a young plant to help it grow — 培 means to cultivate and nurture.

sentence.en: The teacher nurtures our love of reading.

批.origin: A hand 扌 next to 比 (compare) — looking closely to compare and judge, so 批 means to comment or criticize.

聂.origin: One ear 耳 with a pair 双 more below — so many ears leaning in to listen, that's 聂, to whisper.

灵.origin: A hand 彐 over fire 火 — quick and lively like a darting flame, so 灵 means spirit and clever.

琴.origin: Two jade 玨 strings on top and 今 for the sound — 琴 is a beautiful string instrument.

sentence.en: My older sister can play the piano.

渐.origin: Water 氵 beside 斩 for the sound — like a stream slowly rising, 渐 means gradually.

舍.origin: A roof 人 over a space where you speak and rest 舌 — a home, so 舍 means house or dwelling.

sentence.en: I live in the school dormitory.

创.origin: 仓 (storehouse) beside a knife 刂 — carving something new, so 创 means to create and establish.

杰.origin: A tree 木 standing tall over a fire 灬 — outstanding above the rest, so 杰 means a hero.

侵.origin: A person 亻 sneaking in with a sweeping hand — pushing into another's space, so 侵 means to invade.

愤.origin: The heart 忄 beside 贲 for sound — a heart bursting hot inside is 愤, anger.

sentence.en: He was so furious he was shaking.

义.origin: A simple cross 乂 with a dot 丶 on top — doing what is fair and right, that's 义.

鼓.origin: 壴 shows a tall drum on a stand and 支 is a hand with a stick — 鼓 is a drum, ready to be hit.

奋.origin: A big person 大 working hard in the field 田 — putting in real effort, so 奋 means to strive.

sentence.en: We must work hard and strive.

抗.origin: A hand 扌 pushing back against 亢 — standing firm with your hands up, so 抗 means to resist.
